name: Michigan Central Sch Bus LLC
recent_news: No public news found; small operator with no website or web footprint located in Downriver Detroit area. Note: address 4324 13th St Wyandotte is on same street as (but distinct from) Trinity Transportation at 4624 13th St, which is the dominant Wayne County special-ed contractor.
fit_notes: Possible micro tuck-in if it is in fact an independent yellow-bus contractor (~41 employees implies ~20-30 bus fleet), but no web presence makes diligence difficult. Likely overshadowed by Trinity Transportation locally; worth a direct phone call to confirm operator status before further work.

name: T2's Bus Svc
recent_news: D&B lists 1 employee and ~$25K revenue under SIC 'Services NEC' (not school transportation). No school-bus or transportation operating evidence found. New Orleans charter/school bus market is dominated by Hotard, A&S Transportation (National Express/Summit) and InspireNOLA charter-school in-house fleets.
fit_notes: Likely NOT a viable tuck-in. Public records suggest a 1-person operation with no school-bus classification; the source-list employee count (17) and revenue ($1.94M) are not corroborated. Recommend de-prioritize pending phone confirmation.

name: Iguala Bus Co
recent_news: Address (3321 McKinney St, East End Houston) and name (Iguala = city in Guerrero, Mexico) strongly indicate an intercity US-Mexico charter/line-haul operator, not a school bus contractor. A related Houston-based operator, Iguala BusMex Inc., was declared an imminent hazard and ordered to cease operations by FMCSA in Aug 2008 following a fatal crash on Highway 75 near Sherman, TX. Houston ISD and surrounding districts run their own yellow-bus fleets or use First Student.
fit_notes: NOT a Summit tuck-in. Almost certainly intercity Mexico-route motorcoach service mis-flagged as 'school' in the source list. Possible regulatory/safety baggage given the related Iguala BusMex shutdown. Drop from pipeline.

name: Delaware City Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely Washington Township SD (Sewell) and adjacent Gloucester County districts; NJSBCA member
recent_news: Active NJ school bus contractor and member of the NJ School Bus Contractors Association. D&B/Manta list ~50 employees and ~$1.58M revenue; independent and based at 281 Delsea Dr, Sewell. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Best fit in this batch: independent NJ contractor in southern NJ (Gloucester County), in Summit's existing footprint. ~25-bus scale and 2013 founding make it a credible single-yard tuck-in. Worth a direct outreach to owner; confirm ownership and SPED/contract exposure (Washington Twp, Pitman, Clearview).

name: School Bus Stops Of AL Inc
recent_news: No web presence located. Address (1019A Old Monrovia Rd NW Suite 208, Huntsville) is an office-suite, atypical for a yellow-bus contractor with a yard/fleet. Huntsville City Schools' contracted operator is First Student (formerly AppleBus), not this entity. Name 'School Bus Stops' suggests possible routing/consulting/stop-signage business rather than a contractor.
fit_notes: Likely NOT a school-bus operator/tuck-in target. Office-suite location, no fleet evidence, and Huntsville district already contracted to First Student suggest this is a routing-services, signage, or non-operator entity. Recommend phone-screen Jerry Smith to confirm before any further work; if not an operator, drop.
